steps.m: to be distracted or diverted by amusement

steps.n: Changed from root 'to be distracted' to 'for her/it' - the word is actually a preposition 'for' (لَ) attached to 'her/it' (ها)
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'causing' something - so it changes from 'being distracted' to 'making others distracted'. The ى shows it's past tense.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long aa sound
Added كُمُ at the end to show who was distracted - 'you all'. This word is being acted on by التَّكَاثُرُ (competition in worldly increase) in this ayah.

steps.m: to increase, to multiply, to become more

steps.n: The vowel pattern َُ shows something naturally grew or increased by itself

steps.m: he tried to outdo (someone) in increasing
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show two people/groups competing with each other

steps.m: competing with each other in increasing
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show people doing this action together

steps.m: the act of competing with each other in increasing
steps.n: Removed the last 'a' sound to change it from an action (verb) to a thing/concept (noun)

steps.m: the competing with each other in increasing
steps.n: Added ال ('the') to make it specific - like changing from 'competition' to 'the competition'

steps.m: the competing with each other in increasing
steps.n: Added ُ at end to show this is the main subject of the sentence
This is the basic form of the word that shows when something ends or reaches its limit
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the 't' sound stronger, which helps emphasize the meaning of reaching an endpoint

steps.m: until, up to (shows the end point of زُرْتُمُ - your visiting)
steps.n: The ىٰ at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'your visiting' (زُرْتُمُ) to show when that visiting ends.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make the basic command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a weak letter.
Added تُمُ to show that multiple people (all male or mixed) did the action in the past. This verb comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) in the ayah.
The suffix تُمُ shows that multiple people (all male or mixed) did the action
Added مَ at the beginning and made the middle letter quiet with ْ to change from the action of burying to the place where burial happens
Changed from single graveyard to many graveyards by adding 'aa' after ق and 'i' before ر

steps.m: the burial places/graveyards (where زُرْتُمُ/you visited happens)
steps.n: Adding definite article makes the noun specific

steps.m: the burial places/graveyards (object of زُرْتُمُ/you visited)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is what is being visited in the sentence
Added fatha and shadda to make the basic negative particle. The shadda (doubled letter) makes it stronger.

steps.m: No indeed! (used for strong denial or rejection)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to create the complete emphatic particle. This word appears in the Quran to strongly reject or deny something that was mentioned before.
This is the basic form of the future particle that shows something will happen later
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. When used with تَعْلَمُونَ (you know), it means 'you will know' with emphasis that this will definitely happen in the future.
Added vowels to connect the letters together. The َ (fatha) and ِ (kasra) show this is about something that happened in the past.
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added ون to show many people are doing it. This word comes after سَوْفَ which means it will happen soon.
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else
The damma (ُ) is just showing how we say this word - it's always pronounced 'thumma', meaning 'then'

steps.m: then, next, afterwards (shows sequence of events in relation to سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ - you will know)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger and connects it to what comes next. In this ayah, it connects to سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (you will know), showing these events will happen one after another.
This is a strong word of denial. The shadda (doubled ل) makes it more emphatic than a simple 'no'.
Adding the shadda (doubled ل) makes the word stronger, like saying something with extra force

steps.m: no indeed, absolutely not (strong denial of what came before ثُمَّ)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end makes this word specifically used for strong denial or rejection. In this ayah, it's rejecting whatever was mentioned after ثُمَّ (then).
This is a future particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) happen in the more distant future. It's stronger than just using س- at the beginning of a word.
Connected the root letters together with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'. The vowel pattern change also helps show it's happening now.
Adding ون at the end makes it plural - now talking to many people. This verb comes after سَوْفَ which makes it future tense.
This is the basic part that shows strong disagreement or rejection
Adding ل with shadda makes the rejection stronger

steps.m: No indeed! By no means! (followed by لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ - if you only knew)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes this a complete particle of strong denial. In this ayah, it's followed by 'if you only knew', showing that people are being warned about something important they're not realizing.
This is a particle that expresses a wish or regret about something that didn't happen. Here it shows regret about not having certain knowledge.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'

steps.m: you all know (referring to the knowledge of certainty عِلْمَ ٱلْيَقِينِ)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is addressing multiple people. This verb connects to عِلْمَ ٱلْيَقِينِ (knowledge of certainty) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to know, have knowledge of something

steps.n: Changed from a verb (to know) into a noun (knowledge). This word is the object of تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) in this ayah.
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know)
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it a noun meaning the state of being certain
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to the word عِلْمَ (knowledge) that comes before it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' as the doer, and the weak letter changes at the end
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis and future meaning

steps.m: surely you all will see (with strong emphasis)
steps.n: Added نّ with shadda at the end for extra emphasis
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show intense quality

steps.m: the Hellfire (object of لَتَرَوُنَّ - you will surely see)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the Hellfire') and َ to show it's the object being seen. This word is what will be seen in the phrase 'you will surely see'
This is the basic form of the word that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then (connects to لَتَرَوُنَّهَا - you will surely see it)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) on ث and ّ (shadda) on م to strengthen the meaning. This word connects to what comes next - that you will surely see it.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root 'to see' into 'he saw' in past tense. The last letter changes to ى (alif maqsurah) in this verb.
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' as the doer. The أ drops due to Arabic morphology rules.
Added لَ at start to emphasize 'certainly/indeed you will see'
Added و and نّ to show plural 'you all' with emphasis. The ى changes to و before نّ.

steps.m: surely you all will see it (عَيْنَ ٱلْيَقِينِ)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is being seen (refers to عَيْنَ ٱلْيَقِينِ - the certainty)

steps.m: eye (object of لَتَرَوُنَّهَا - you will surely see it)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the noun 'eye'. This word is what is being seen in the phrase 'you will surely see it'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added ي to make it into a noun meaning the state or quality of certainty
Added ال to make it specific - this refers to complete and perfect certainty
Added kasra at the end to show this describes the word عَيْنَ (eye) in the phrase 'eye of certainty'

steps.m: then (connects to لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ - you will surely be asked)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to the next statement about being questioned.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he asked' to 'you are being asked' by adding تُ at the start and changing the vowels to show someone is receiving the action
Added لَ at start to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: you will definitely be asked (about النَّعِيمِ/the blessings)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to make it extra certain. This word connects to النَّعِيمِ (the blessings) which is what will be asked about.
The middle letter و is weak but stays because it's essential to the meaning
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha (small line) at the end because this word is connected to what comes after it, showing 'the day of...'

steps.m: on that day (when you will be asked about pleasure)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which means 'that' or 'at that time'. This refers to the day of judgment when people will be asked about their blessings, as mentioned in the surrounding ayah.

steps.m: about/concerning (connects to لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ - you will be asked)
steps.n: Basic preposition that shows what topic or subject is being discussed. In this ayah, it connects to the verb لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ (you will be asked) to show what you'll be asked about.

steps.m: about/concerning (connects to لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ - you will be asked)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it's followed by the word ٱلنَّعِيمِ (the pleasure/comfort). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (doing something) to a noun meaning lots of comfort/blessing
Added definite article and genitive case ending
